video_title: A Deadly American Marriage | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
The recordings don’t reveal domestic abuse—they sound like typical arguments between parents. There’s no screaming, no cursing, no sounds of violence. And let’s not forget: Molly knew she was recording, he didn’t. It’s entirely possible she spoke in a calm, calculated way to paint herself as the innocent party, knowing she would use these recordings later.

I still can’t believe the original verdict was overturned. Even the kids said they were coached by Molly—with help from Molly's father, a former FBI agent. The crime scene alone tells the real story: they killed him. The physical evidence is overwhelming, and the interviews Molly and her father gave at the police station only sealed their guilt. They lied repeatedly—on camera, in statements, everywhere.

There were no serious injuries on Molly or her dad to back up their claim that he attacked her. And the toxicology report showed that he was not drunk at all. If Molly and her Dad had any injuries at all, it’s entirely plausible they inflicted them on themselves to sell the story. And the mother saying she fell back asleep after hearing screams? Come on. That alone is beyond suspicious as well as the fact that she never spoke on this documentary.

Molly and her father took this man’s life. They should be serving 20+ year sentences. Shame on the prosecution for offering a plea deal, and shame on the judge for only adding only a few extra months after hearing the impact statements from the kids. This is one of the most egregious miscarriages of justice I’ve ever seen. The physical evidence screams guilt—and yet somehow, they walked.

video_title: A Deadly American Marriage | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
From all the information presented in the documentary, it appears Tom and Molly have abused the justice system and exploited the former FBI officer's counterintelligence training; the smokescreen they throw up in the face of overwhelming evidence exposing untruths, cover ups, the father's intention to return to Ireland, and the children's accounts of emotional abuse, is horrifying. No one sleeps with a brick on their nightstand without intent to do harm, and it would not take lethal force to neutralise.

Tom and Molly may feel too far in to let go of the lie, but the evidence speaks volumes, and it seems their priority has been misdirection. The claims of Molly's instability coincide with her detached demeanor and rhetoric and apathetic delivery throughout the documentary. Such a heartbreaking series of events.

Offering a solitary modicum of solace is that the children appear to have been raised in a genuine, compassionate, and supportive familial environment where they can know love and community, and cultivate resilience.

video_title: A Deadly American Marriage | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: Of course, a former FBI officer being arrested for murder would not look good for the institution's image. So, they managed to turn the situation around, turning the victim into a defendant. Based on the evidence in the photos, you don't need to be an expert to conclude that the victim was beaten to death. There are clear signs of uncontrollable rage, given the amount of blood found in the rooms. In a possible defense, one or two blows with the baseball bat would stop the possible attacker. However, that was not what happened, according to the coroner. The story of the brick leads one to believe that it could have been a weapon that could have been used at some point. Who keeps a brick at the head of their bed? In addition, analyzing the body language of the father and daughter, one gets the impression that there are many more untruths than truths. In my opinion, the two defendants were responsible for the boy's death. That's my opinion. But there's a lot going on when the accused is a former police officer, especially from an institution with a name to uphold.
